title: UDS grammar manifest
description: The studio's adoption layer for the nopilot Universal Design System (ADR-006). The concrete token VALUES live in a W3C Design Tokens file (tokens.yaml, validated by Style Dictionary) in the brand store; THIS schema validates the brand-agnostic grammar the studio adds on top: the format set, the cross-format px→pt type rule, the aspect classes, the markdown mapping, and the contract register (archetypes). Authored across uds.yml + aspect-classes.yml + archetypes.yml and merged by studio.uds.load_uds().

token_format.description: The token source format. The UDS source of truth is W3C Design Tokens.

transform.description: How tokens.yaml is transformed into per-format artifacts.

tokens_source.description: Brand-store-relative path to the W3C tokens file (e.g. '{brand}/tokens.yaml').

type_px_to_pt.description: The cross-format type rule: each size token's web px maps to a fixed document pt, so a heading is the same heading in a browser or a slide. Keys are font.size names in tokens.yaml.

render_roles.description: House-style render roles — the cross-format formatting contract. Each generic role maps to one font.size token (resolved px/pt per aspect class) plus the weight/transform/alignment/colour it is set in. A serialiser maps its constructs to these roles and resolves values from the brand's tokens.yaml. Read by studio.uds.render_role().

type.description: A font.size token name (resolved to px/pt per aspect class).

weight.description: A font.weight token name; omit for the serialiser default.

profiles.description: Render profiles — the purpose layer over an aspect class (e.g. presentation vs proposal). Each selects an aspect, a column count, optional pagination, and optional absolute per-role size overrides (pt) that win over the aspect type_scale. Read by studio.uds.profile_spec() / render_role(profile=...).

table_size.description: Table cell pt for this profile.

group_lead.description: Paragraphs of supporting prose that group onto a table/diagram slide (instead of a separate slide).

sizes.description: Absolute pt per render role; overrides the aspect type_scale.

aspect_classes.description: The media classes a docket renders across. Each format maps to one; the px→pt rule applies to pt-unit classes.

unit.description: Type/length unit for this class. px → screen; pt → document/slide (apply type_px_to_pt).

type_scale.description: Multiplier on the pt type scale for this class (slides step up for distance; page = 1.0 baseline).

markdown_mapping.description: The spine: each markdown construct → one element per format. Every construct has exactly one defined home.

seal_rule.description: The governing rule: archetypes may be extended and branded, never altered.

archetypes.description: The contract register, by tier: infrastructure (page/app scaffold) · view (content arrangement) · component (composable block) · element (atomic). Each is a strict contract — extend + brand, never alter.

formats.description: Formats the contract must render in (app-structure tiers are typically [html]).

sealed.description: Contract terms that cannot be altered (only extended/branded); changing one forks.

extends.description: The open surface — where a design may extend without forking.

item.description: For listing views: the archetype used as the repeated item (e.g. card).

links_to.description: The archetype this opens to (e.g. card / lister → detail).

variants.description: Named variant axes (e.g. lister arrangement / cardinality).

reflow.description: Optional per-aspect-class layout note (keyed by an aspect_classes name).
